title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
prompt:
Given an unsorted array of integers `nums`, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence.

You must write an algorithm that runs in O(n) time.

examples.output:
4
Explanation: The longest consecutive sequence is [1, 2, 3, 4]. Its length is 4.

9
Explanation: The longest consecutive sequence is [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]. Its length is 9.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Put all numbers in a HashSet. For each number n, only start counting a sequence if n-1 is NOT in the set (this means n is the start of a sequence). Then count upward (n+1, n+2, ...) while elements exist in the set. Track the maximum length.
commonBugs: Starting a sequence from every number (leads to O(n^2) instead of O(n))
Not handling duplicates (use a set to deduplicate)
Not handling the empty array edge case
